name: Oysters Rockefeller (Crocker)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Chopped fresh or frozen spinach, partially thawed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill three pie plates, 9x 1 1/4 inches, 1/2 inch deep with rock salt.
2. Sprinkle each plate with water.
3. Scrub oysters in shells under running cold water.
4. Break off the thin end of the shell with a hammer.
5. Force a table knife or shucking knife between the halves of the shell at the broken end; pull apart.
6. Cut the oyster at the muscle to separate from the shell.
7. Remove any bits of shell.
8. Place the oyster on the deep half of the shell; discard the other half.
9. Arrange filled shells on rock salt base.
10. Preheat oven to 450°F.
11. Heat margarine in a pan over medium heat. Add onion, parsley, and celery; cook until onion is tender.
12. Mix in the remaining ingredients (spinach mixture).
13. Spoon about 1 tablespoon of spinach mixture onto each oyster.
14. Bake for 10 minutes.
15. Serve with 2 servings; 355 calories per serving.
Oysters Parmesan: Omit spinach mixture. Spoon 1 teaspoon sour cream onto each oyster.
Mix 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese, 1/4 cup cracker crumbs, 1/4 cup melted margarine or butter, and 1/2 teaspoon dry mustard.
Spoon about 2 teaspoons of cheese mixture onto each oyster.
